fault_hypothesis: The overlap check on line 62 uses strict inequalities (`b[1] <= a[0] or b[0] >= a[1]`), which treats touching boundaries (e.g., b[1]==a[0] or b[0]==a[1]) as non-overlapping. The tests expect touching boundaries to be treated as overlapping, so the condition should use strict less-than (`b[1] < a[0] or b[0] > a[1]`).

spec.expr: not (candidate[1] <= slot[0] or candidate[0] >= slot[1]) == (not (candidate[1] < slot[0] or candidate[0] > slot[1]))

constraints.intent: At the overlap check line, the strict-inequality version and the correct boundary-inclusive version should agree — they differ when boundaries touch, revealing the bug.

spec.expr: not (candidate[1] == slot[0] or candidate[0] == slot[1]) or (not (candidate[1] <= slot[0] or candidate[0] >= slot[1]))

constraints.intent: If candidate touches slot at a boundary, the overlaps function must have returned True (i.e., we should not still be iterating); this is violated when boundary touch is incorrectly treated as non-overlapping.

spec.expr: all((candidate[1] < s[0] or candidate[0] > s[1]) for s in existing)

constraints.intent: Before merged is constructed, every existing slot must be strictly non-touching with candidate; if any boundary was touched the candidate should have been rejected already.
When overlaps returns False (else branch of if overlaps), candidate and slot must be strictly non-adjacent — not merely non-overlapping under the lenient boundary rule.
